data.title: Archimedes Tech SPAC Partners II Announces Forge Nano Merger
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Archimedes Tech SPAC Partners II Corporation ( (ATII) ) has shared an update.

On April 20, 2026, Archimedes Tech SPAC Partners II entered into an agreement to merge with Forge Nano in a multi-step business combination that will re-domicile the SPAC from the Cayman Islands to Delaware, create a new holding company called Forge Nano Holdings Inc., and value Forge Nano at approximately $1.2 billion in equity consideration, with existing Forge Nano shareholders receiving Pubco stock and potential earn-out of up to 90 million additional shares tied to share price and revenue milestones over five years. The deal, expected to close as early as the third quarter of 2026 subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals, is supported by a $100 million PIPE financing featuring 10 million new shares and 15 million warrants plus anti-dilution and reset mechanics, sponsor share contributions to backstop additional financing, and lock-up and registration rights agreements that will concentrate post-closing control with Forge Nano holders and provide substantial upside incentives but also introduce meaningful warrant overhang and potential dilution for public investors.

Conditions to closing include effectiveness of an S-4 registration statement, Hart-Scott-Rodino clearance, Nasdaq listing approval for the new Pubco shares, and the absence of material adverse effects on either party, while either side can terminate the agreement under a range of failure-to-close, breach or regulatory-block triggers before the outside date. Governance at closing will shift to an eight-member board with seven directors designated by Forge Nano and one by the SPAC sponsor, aligning corporate control with the operating target, and a comprehensive package of lock-up restrictions, registration rights and warrant features will shape trading dynamics, capital-raising flexibility and ownership concentration in the newly listed Forge Nano Holdings Inc. following completion of the transaction.

More about Archimedes Tech SPAC Partners II Corporation

Archimedes Tech SPAC Partners II Corporation is a Cayman Islands-incorporated special purpose acquisition company focused on effecting mergers, share exchanges and similar business combinations to take private companies public, with its securities listed on Nasdaq. Through its proposed merger with U.S.-based Forge Nano, Inc., a technology company, Archimedes Tech SPAC Partners II aims to re-domicile in Delaware and transition into an operating holding company under the name Forge Nano Holdings Inc., shifting from a capital-raising shell to an advanced industrial technology platform for public market investors.

Average Trading Volume: 54,233

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $308.9M

Learn more about ATII stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.description:

Apple ( (AAPL) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On April 20, 2026, Apple announced that Tim Cook will step down as Chief Executive Officer and become Executive Chair of the Board, effective September 1, 2026, marking a major leadership transition at the company. The Board has appointed John Ternus, currently Senior Vice President of Hardware Engineering and a long-time Apple executive since 2001, as CEO and Board member from the same date, while current Chair Art Levinson will assume the role of Lead Independent Director.

Apple highlighted Ternus’s deep experience in hardware engineering, product design, and innovation as key strengths expected to shape its future strategy and operations. The company also disclosed that Ternus has no disclosable family or related-party transactions with Apple’s leadership and will enter into Apple’s standard indemnification agreement for directors and executive officers, signaling a routine but closely watched governance shift for investors and other stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (AAPL) stock is a Buy with a $315.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Apple stock, see the AAPL Stock Forecast page.

The score is driven primarily by exceptional profitability and cash-flow strength, reinforced by upbeat earnings-call results and guidance. Offsetting factors are balance-sheet leverage risk and a premium valuation with a low dividend yield; technicals are supportive but not strongly momentum-driven.

To see Spark’s full report on AAPL stock, click here.

The company’s flagship products, including the iPhone, Mac, iPad, and Apple Watch, along with its services ecosystem, position Apple as a leading player in the consumer electronics and digital services markets.

data.title: OneMedNet Faces Nasdaq Non-Compliance Over Minimum Bid Price
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

OneMedNet ( (ONMD) ) has issued an announcement.

On April 14, 2026, OneMedNet disclosed that it had received a notice from Nasdaq staff stating that the company is no longer in compliance with the exchange’s $1.00 minimum bid price requirement, after its common stock traded below that threshold for 30 consecutive business days. Under Nasdaq rules, OneMedNet now has 180 calendar days, until October 12, 2026, to lift its share price to at least $1.00 for ten straight trading days, and could receive a further 180-day extension if it meets other listing standards and commits to a remedial plan.

The company said it will monitor its share price and may pursue options such as a reverse stock split to regain compliance, but cautioned that there is no guarantee it will succeed, and failure to meet the bid price or other Nasdaq requirements could lead to the stock being delisted from the Nasdaq Capital Market, potentially reducing liquidity and access to capital for shareholders and the business.

More about OneMedNet

OneMedNet Corporation operates in the healthcare and medical data technology sector, focusing on solutions that leverage clinical data and imaging for research, diagnostics, or related healthcare applications. The company’s shares are listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market, giving it access to public equity investors and subjecting it to Nasdaq’s continued listing requirements.

data.title: NRC Health Adopts New Name and Governance Structure
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from National Research ( (NRC) ).

On April 15, 2026, National Research Corporation formally changed its corporate name to NRC Health through a charter amendment filed in Delaware, aligning its legal identity with the brand it has used since 2016 while keeping its NASDAQ ticker symbol “NRC” unchanged and leaving shareholder rights unaffected. The board simultaneously adopted amended and restated bylaws that, among other technical and administrative updates, declassify the board so all directors stand for annual election starting with the June 23, 2026 annual meeting, set a 12-member board cap, define the chair’s role, and adjust procedures and deadlines for shareholder meetings, nominations, and proposals, with current directors’ term-shortening resignations framed as a procedural step rather than a governance dispute.

These governance changes mark a shift to a more annually accountable board structure, bringing NRC Health’s corporate framework closer to prevailing public-company governance norms and clarifying shareholder engagement processes. For investors and other stakeholders, the combination of the name change and bylaw revisions signals an effort to strengthen brand consistency while enhancing transparency and predictability around board elections and shareholder participation.

Spark’s Take on NRC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, NRC is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held back by deteriorating financial performance (declining revenue and margin compression) and elevated balance-sheet leverage, alongside bearish technical momentum (price below key moving averages and negative MACD). These negatives are only partially offset by positive free cash flow, a ~4% dividend yield, and a constructive corporate update showing record recurring contract value growth.

To see Spark’s full report on NRC stock, click here.

More about National Research

National Research Corporation, which now operates under the name NRC Health, is a NASDAQ-listed company focused on measuring and improving healthcare experiences under the NRC brand it has used since 2016. The business operates within the health services and analytics space, using its NRC identity in the market while maintaining the ticker symbol “NRC” for investors and other market participants.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

TLGY Acquisition ( (TLGYF) ) has issued an update.

On April 15, 2026, TLGY Acquisition Corporation shareholders approved an amendment to its governing documents to reduce the monthly amount its sponsors must deposit into the trust account to extend the deadline for completing an initial business combination, lowering the cap from $25,000 to $10,000 per month while maintaining a potential six one-month extensions. The meeting had a quorum with about 91% of eligible ordinary shares represented, the extension proposal passed overwhelmingly, a small number of Class A shareholders redeemed their shares, and following the April 16, 2026 filing of the amendment in the Cayman Islands, TLGY’s sponsors deposited $10,000 to extend the termination date of the SPAC by one month from April 29, 2026 to May 29, 2026.

The vote underscores shareholder support for giving TLGY more time and flexibility to complete a business combination on more cost-efficient terms for its sponsors, while only a limited portion of public shareholders opted for redemption, suggesting continued investor willingness to remain in the vehicle as it seeks a target.

More about TLGY Acquisition

TLGY Acquisition Corporation is a Cayman Islands incorporated special purpose acquisition company, or SPAC, formed to identify and consummate an initial business combination. The company holds investors’ capital in a trust account while seeking a suitable target and operates within the broader blank-check and capital markets deal-making ecosystem.

On April 16, 2026, La Rosa Holdings Corp. announced it would implement a 1-for-10 reverse stock split of its common shares, effective April 20, 2026 at 12:01 a.m. Eastern Time, with the stock continuing to trade on Nasdaq under the LRHC ticker on a split-adjusted basis and a new CUSIP number assigned to the shares. data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

NXP Semiconductors ( (NXPI) ) has shared an announcement.

On April 20, 2026, NXP Semiconductors announced that subsidiaries NXP B.V., NXP USA Inc. and NXP Funding LLC fully redeemed US$750 million of 3.875% senior notes originally due in June 2026, retiring the debt in line with the terms of the indenture. The move comes as the Netherlands-based chipmaker continues to serve automotive, industrial and IoT, mobile and communications infrastructure customers across more than 30 countries.

Chief financial officer Bill Betz framed the redemption as part of NXP’s disciplined capital allocation strategy, alongside ongoing share buybacks, regular dividends and active balance-sheet management. By eliminating a sizable upcoming maturity, the company is tightening control of its capital structure, which could modestly reduce interest expense and support flexibility for shareholder returns and strategic investments.

The transaction underscores NXP’s effort to maintain a conservative financial profile amid shifting demand cycles in the global semiconductor market. For bondholders, the early redemption crystallizes returns ahead of the original June 2026 maturity, while equity investors may view the move as a signal of confidence in cash generation and balance-sheet strength.

The most recent analyst rating on (NXPI) stock is a Hold with a $235.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on NXP Semiconductors stock, see the NXPI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on NXPI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, NXPI is a Neutral.

The score is led by solid underlying fundamentals—strong margins and robust free-cash-flow generation—tempered by leverage and muted recent growth. Technicals are the biggest near-term drag, with the stock below key moving averages and negative momentum. Valuation is fair-to-slightly-rich (P/E ~27) but supported by a ~2% dividend, while earnings-call guidance was constructive yet balanced by inventory, communications-infrastructure weakness, and near-term cash outlays.

To see Spark’s full report on NXPI stock, click here.

More about NXP Semiconductors

NXP Semiconductors N.V. is a global semiconductor manufacturer serving the automotive, industrial and IoT, mobile, and communications infrastructure markets. The company focuses on system solutions that enhance connectivity, safety, and security, operates in more than 30 countries, and generated $12.27 billion in revenue in 2025.

Positioning itself as a trusted partner for innovative electronics, NXP leverages its “Brighter Together” approach, combining advanced chip technologies with engineering expertise. This strategy supports its role as a key supplier to automakers, industrial customers, and mobile and network equipment makers worldwide.

NXP’s broad geographic footprint and diversified end markets help balance exposure to cyclical swings in individual sectors. Its emphasis on secure, connected solutions aligns with long-term trends in vehicle electrification, factory automation, smart devices, and advanced communications infrastructure.

data.title: RBB Bancorp Details Strong First-Quarter 2026 Financial Performance
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Rbb Bancorp ( (RBB) ) has provided an update.

RBB Bancorp reported solid first-quarter 2026 results on April 20, with net income rising to $11.3 million, or $0.66 per diluted share, driven by higher net interest income, an expanded net interest margin of 3.15% and improved credit quality as nonperforming assets fell 9% to $48.8 million. The company also declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.16 per share payable on May 15, 2026, while maintaining stable asset and deposit levels, underscoring management’s focus on disciplined loan growth, retail deposit gathering and problem asset resolution to support shareholder returns.

Net interest income increased to $30.5 million despite fewer days in the quarter, as lower funding costs and a special Federal Home Loan Bank dividend helped offset modest loan income pressure. Noninterest income improved on higher gains from other real estate owned and recoveries on acquired loans, efficiency strengthened with the ratio improving to 55.41%, and loans and retail deposits both grew year over year, highlighting a more resilient balance sheet heading into the rest of 2026.

On April 21, 2026, RBB Bancorp planned to host a conference call to discuss its results for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 and other company matters. Supporting presentation materials with historical information were made available on the company’s website to provide additional context for investors and analysts reviewing its first-quarter performance.

Radware ( (RDWR) ) just unveiled an update.

Radware announced on April 20, 2026 that it will hold its 2026 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders on May 25, 2026 at its U.S. offices in Mahwah, New Jersey, with April 21, 2026 set as the record date for shareholder participation. The agenda includes electing three Class III directors through 2029, approving equity-based awards and bonus structure changes for the CEO, and reappointing Ernst & Young-affiliated Kost Forer Gabbay & Kasierer as auditors, underscoring ongoing governance, executive compensation oversight and financial reporting continuity for investors.

Shareholders will also review the company’s financial statements and auditors’ report for the year ended December 31, 2025, and may address additional business that properly comes before the meeting. Voting on all proposals will be decided by a simple majority of shares present or represented by proxy, with added approval thresholds for the CEO compensation item under Israeli corporate law, and a quorum will require holders of at least 35% of voting power, highlighting the company’s adherence to both Nasdaq and Israeli regulatory governance standards.

An update from Dell Technologies ( (DELL) ) is now available.

On multiple dates between March 2 and April 16, 2026, Dell Technologies issued 4,237,699 shares of its Class C common stock upon the conversion of an equal number of Class B shares held by Silver Lake-affiliated funds, leaving 325,654,621 Class C shares and 47,789,758 Class B shares outstanding as of April 17, 2026. The conversions, executed on a one-to-one basis under Dell’s charter and relying on a Securities Act exemption without payment of commissions, shift part of the company’s equity base from Class B to the more widely held Class C stock, clarifying the capital structure for investors while preserving identical economic rights between the two classes.

Westrock Coffee ( (WEST) ) has issued an update.

On April 20, 2026, Westrock Coffee Company appointed A. Wellford Tabor to its Board of Directors and to the Audit & Finance Committee, filling a Class II seat vacated following the previously disclosed retirement of director R. Brad Martin, whose term runs through the 2027 annual shareholder meeting. The Board also reduced its size by one seat to remove the vacancy arising from the retirement of director Josie Natori, aligning board size with current membership.

Tabor was designated by the RVAC Majority under an investor rights agreement that allows this shareholder group to appoint representatives as long as it maintains at least a 10% stake in Westrock Coffee. As Head of Direct Investments and a managing director at HF Capital, which manages a vehicle holding more than 10% of Westrock’s common stock, and as a holder of the company’s 5.00% convertible senior notes due 2029 and 2031, Tabor’s appointment further embeds a major investor’s influence in the boardroom while maintaining standard non‑employee director compensation and indemnification terms.

data.title: Trade Desk Expands and Extends Revolving Credit Facility
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Trade Desk ( (TTD) ) has issued an update.

On April 14, 2026, The Trade Desk entered into an amended and restated loan and security agreement that refinances and extends its revolving credit facility with a bank syndicate led by JPMorgan Chase to a new maturity date of April 14, 2031. The revised arrangement provides a $750 million revolving line with additional sublimits for letters of credit and swingline loans, and includes an option to expand total capacity by up to an additional $750 million under certain conditions.

The facility is secured by substantially all of the company’s assets, with potential collateral release upon achieving specified investment-grade ratings, and loans will bear interest at variable base rate or term SOFR-based pricing with margins tied to The Trade Desk’s net leverage ratio. The agreement imposes customary covenants and leverage tests, including a maximum consolidated funded debt-to-EBITDA ratio of 3.50 to 1.00 with a temporary step-up permitted for material transactions, which together are likely to enhance the company’s financial flexibility while preserving lender protections and signaling a robust banking relationship that supports its long-term growth plans.

The most recent analyst rating on (TTD) stock is a Buy with a $32.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Trade Desk stock, see the TTD Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TTD is a Outperform.
